title: Are You A Baker Or A Faker?
description: Are You A Baker Or A Faker? — fun, shareable, and packed with surprising facts.

questions.text: What makes bread rise?
questions.description: Yeast ferments sugars to produce CO₂, which makes dough expand.

questions.text: Which ingredient adds flakiness to pastries?
questions.description: Cold fat creates layers that puff when baked.

questions.text: What does al dente mean for pasta?
questions.description: It means the pasta is cooked but still firm to the bite.

questions.text: Which spice gives curry its yellow color?
questions.description: Turmeric provides the signature golden hue.

questions.text: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
questions.description: Ripe avocados are mashed to make guacamole.

questions.text: Which cooking method uses dry heat in an oven?
questions.description: Baking uses surrounding dry heat to cook food.

questions.text: What does sauté mean?
questions.description: To cook quickly in a small amount of oil over high heat.

options.text: Cook quickly in a little oil

questions.text: Which flour has the highest protein for bread?
questions.description: Bread flour forms stronger gluten networks.

questions.text: What is tempering chocolate used for?
questions.description: It stabilizes cocoa butter for a glossy snap finish.

questions.text: What does resting dough do?
questions.description: It relaxes gluten and improves texture and elasticity.

questions.text: What happens if you over-knead bread dough?
questions.description: Over-kneading can make dough too tight and result in dense bread.

questions.text: Why is cold butter used in pastry making?
questions.description: Cold butter creates layers that produce a flaky texture.

questions.text: What does baking powder do in recipes?
questions.description: It releases gas that helps baked goods rise.

questions.text: Why do cookies spread too much while baking?
questions.description: Too much butter or low flour can cause spreading.

questions.text: What is the purpose of preheating an oven?
questions.description: It ensures consistent baking temperature from the start.

questions.text: What happens if you skip preheating the oven?
questions.description: Baked goods may cook unevenly or fail to rise properly.

questions.text: Why is measuring ingredients accurately important in baking?
questions.description: Baking is precise, and small changes affect results.

questions.text: What does sugar do in baking besides adding sweetness?
questions.description: Sugar also affects texture and browning.

questions.text: Why do cakes sink in the middle after baking?
questions.description: Often caused by underbaking or opening the oven too early.

questions.text: What is the role of eggs in baking?
questions.description: Eggs provide structure, moisture, and binding.

questions.text: What happens if you use too much baking soda?
questions.description: It can create a bitter taste and uneven texture.

questions.text: Why should ingredients be at room temperature?
questions.description: It helps ingredients mix evenly and improves texture.

questions.text: What is folding in baking?
questions.description: A gentle mixing method to retain air in the batter.

questions.text: Why does bread dough need proofing?
questions.description: Proofing allows yeast to produce gas and expand dough.

options.text: To let yeast create air bubbles

questions.text: What happens if dough is under-proofed?
questions.description: The bread may be dense and not rise properly.

questions.text: What happens if dough is over-proofed?
questions.description: The dough can collapse and lose structure.

questions.text: What is the function of salt in baking?
questions.description: Salt enhances flavor and controls yeast activity.

questions.text: Why is kneading important in bread making?
questions.description: It develops gluten, giving structure to bread.

questions.text: What is gluten?
questions.description: A protein that gives structure and elasticity to dough.

questions.text: Why do cakes need leavening agents?
questions.description: They help cakes rise and become light.

questions.text: What is the purpose of adding mulch around plants?
questions.description: Mulch helps retain moisture, suppress weeds, and regulate soil temperature.

questions.text: Why should you rotate crops in a garden?
questions.description: Crop rotation prevents soil nutrient depletion and reduces pests.

options.text: Maintain soil health and reduce pests

questions.text: What is a common sign of underwatering plants?
questions.description: Dry soil and wilting leaves indicate lack of water.

questions.text: Why is spacing between plants important?
questions.description: Proper spacing allows airflow and reduces disease risk.

questions.text: What does leggy plant growth indicate?
questions.description: Plants become leggy when they lack sufficient light.

questions.text: What is the role of nitrogen in plant growth?
questions.description: Nitrogen promotes healthy leaf and stem growth.

questions.text: Why should you avoid compacted soil?
questions.description: Compacted soil restricts root growth and water absorption.

questions.text: What is deadheading in gardening?
questions.description: Removing spent flowers encourages new blooms.

questions.text: Why is organic fertilizer beneficial?
questions.description: It improves soil structure and adds nutrients naturally.

questions.text: What does root rot usually result from?
questions.description: Root rot is caused by excessive moisture and poor drainage.

questions.text: What is the benefit of raised garden beds?
questions.description: They improve drainage and soil control.

questions.text: Why are earthworms beneficial in soil?
questions.description: They improve soil aeration and nutrient availability.

questions.text: What is companion planting?
questions.description: Growing plants together that benefit each other.

questions.text: Why is pruning roots important when repotting?
questions.description: It encourages new root growth and prevents overcrowding.

questions.text: What does slow plant growth usually indicate?
questions.description: It may indicate lack of nutrients or poor conditions.

questions.text: Why should you avoid watering during strong sunlight?
questions.description: Water evaporates quickly and can scorch leaves.

questions.text: What is the purpose of seed germination?
questions.description: It is the process where seeds begin to grow into plants.

questions.text: What happens if plants receive too much fertilizer?
questions.description: Excess fertilizer can burn roots and damage plants.

questions.text: Why is sunlight direction important in gardening?
questions.description: Different plants require different light exposure.

questions.text: What is the role of phosphorus in plants?
questions.description: Phosphorus supports root development and flowering.

questions.text: Why should tools be cleaned after gardening?
questions.description: Cleaning tools prevents disease spread between plants.

questions.text: What is transplant shock?
questions.description: Stress plants experience after being moved.

questions.text: Why is air circulation important for plants?
questions.description: Good airflow reduces disease and improves growth.

questions.text: What is the benefit of shade for certain plants?
questions.description: Some plants need protection from direct sunlight.

questions.text: Why is repotting necessary for growing plants?
questions.description: It provides more space for roots to expand.

questions.text: What does soil aeration improve?
questions.description: It allows roots to access oxygen more easily.

questions.text: Why is weed control important?
questions.description: Weeds compete with plants for nutrients and water.

questions.text: What is the purpose of greenhouse gardening?
questions.description: It allows controlled growing conditions.

questions.text: Why are seasonal plants important in gardening?
questions.description: They grow best in specific climate conditions.

questions.text: What defines healthy plant growth?
questions.description: Balanced nutrients, proper light, and watering.
